compiled_state.title: Add recipe_type field to recipe-registry JSON schema (generative|transform, optional)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Description
Update recipes/schemas/recipe-registry-schema.json to add an optional recipe_type field. This is a backward-compatible schema extension — S1/S2/S3 registry entries without recipe_type remain valid.

### Schema change:
Add to the recipe entry schema object:
```json
"recipe_type": {
  "type": "string",
  "enum": ["transform", "generative"],
  "description": "transform=modifies existing files (git stash rollback); generative=creates new files (file deletion rollback). Defaults to transform when absent."
}
```
The field is NOT required (omitting it is valid for backward compatibility).

Also update existing S1 entries (add-parameter-python, add-parameter-typescript) with recipe_type=transform for documentation clarity. Update normalize-imports entries similarly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] recipes/schemas/recipe-registry-schema.json contains recipe_type enum definition
  Verify: python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('recipes/schemas/recipe-registry-schema.json')); print(s)"  | grep -q recipe_type || python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('recipes/schemas/recipe-registry-schema.json')); assert 'recipe_type' in str(s)"
- [ ] Existing entries (add-parameter, normalize-imports) still pass schema validation after update
  Verify: python3 -c "import yaml,json,jsonschema; schema=json.load(open('recipes/schemas/recipe-registry-schema.json')); r=yaml.safe_load(open('recipes/recipe-registry.yaml')); [jsonschema.validate(e,schema) for e in r['recipes']]"
- [ ] test-recipe-registry.sh passes (including new recipe_type tests from T-SR-04)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-recipe-registry.sh
